system_prompt: Generate consistent sprite sheet animations for 2D game characters. Maintain identical character design, proportions, and style across all frames.
user_prompt_template:
Create a sprite sheet animation showing a {species} performing this action: {action_description}

Character details:
- Appearance: {appearance}
- Personality: {personality}
- Special ability: {special_ability}

CRITICAL REQUIREMENTS:

1. REFERENCE IMAGE ADHERENCE:
   - Use pixel art style BUT keep the general shape the same as the reference image
   - Maintain the same overall silhouette and proportions as shown in reference
   - Character design should be recognizably the same creature
   - Preserve key features (ears, tail, body shape, etc.) from reference

2. CONSISTENCY:
   - Maintain EXACT same character design in every frame
   - Keep proportions, colors, and features identical across frames
   - Same art style and level of detail throughout
   - Character size must be identical in all frames

3. FULL BODY VISIBILITY:
   - Show the COMPLETE character body (head to feet/tail)
   - DO NOT crop or cut off any body parts
   - DO NOT show only the head or upper body
   - Include all limbs, ears, tail, and features
   - Leave enough space around the character

4. IMAGE FORMAT SPECIFICATIONS:
   - Total image size: EXACTLY 2048Ă—2048 pixels
   - Grid layout: 4 columns Ă— 4 rows = 16 frames total
   - Individual frame size: EXACTLY 512Ă—512 pixels each
   - Grid structure: Frames arranged left-to-right, top-to-bottom
     * Row 1: frames 1-4
     * Row 2: frames 5-8
     * Row 3: frames 9-12
     * Row 4: frames 13-16
   - Frame boundaries: Each frame must be clearly separated with thin black borders (2-3 pixels) for easy extraction
   - No padding: Frames should fill the entire 2048Ă—2048 canvas with no empty margins outside the grid
   - All 16 frames show the complete animation cycle for this single action

5. SMOOTH ANIMATION FLOW:
   - Consecutive frames MUST show gradual, incremental changes in position, pose, or movement
   - Maintain consistent character proportions, colors, and details across ALL 16 frames
   - Motion should flow naturally when frames are played in sequence (1â†’2â†’3...â†’15â†’16)
   - Avoid sudden position jumps, disappearing elements, or visual discontinuities between adjacent frames
   - Each frame represents an equal time interval in the motion cycle
   - Transitions between frames should be smooth enough that the animation appears fluid at 10-15 FPS
   - Distribute the action motion evenly across all 16 frames for maximum smoothness

6. LOOPABLE ANIMATION:
   - Frame 1 (top-left) and frame 16 (bottom-right) must connect seamlessly
   - Animation should cycle smoothly when played on repeat and looped back to start
   - Plan the motion so it returns to starting position after all 16 frames
   - The final pose in frame 16 should naturally transition back to the initial pose in frame 1

7. TECHNICAL SPECS:
   - Pixel art or simple 2D game sprite style
   - Clean white or transparent background inside each frame
   - Character centered in each 512Ă—512 frame cell
   - Motion reflects {personality} personality
   - Equal spacing and consistent grid structure

Generate the complete sprite sheet as a single image with all frames side by side.
notes: This prompt is used for generating sprite animations via image generation APIs like Gemini Flash Image. Emphasizes consistency, full body visibility, and loopable cycles.
